title: Modality & Attachment
text: Modality & Attachment Specify the modality of a form, and whether it is attached or detached to its parent. For non-modal forms, you can override the detached definition at run time by using /attached in the newinstance statement. This switch is ignored if the form is activated as a modal form. Table 1. Values Value Meaning Modal, Attached Modal forms are always attached and have the following behavior: A new instance of the component is a child of the form that created it. When the parent of this component instance closes, this instance, and all of its children are also closed. When the parent of this component instance is iconized, this instance and all of its children are also iconized. Non-Modal, Attached A new instance is a child of the component instance that created it. When the parent of this instance closes, this instance, and all of its children are also closed. Non-Modal, Detached Each new, detached instance remains active until it closes itself or until the application closes. You can override this definition in ProcScript at run time by specifying MODALITY=MODAL with the newinstance ProcScript statement. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
